disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Began 3 months ago as a single rough papule that has multiplied to many lesions across both hands over that period
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and how has it changed over time?
Did it begin as one lump or several?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started about three months ago as just one little rough lump on a finger, and since then it's spread to lots of them on both hands.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lesions are rough, hyperkeratotic, dome-shaped papules with tiny black dots visible within them, disrupting normal skin lines
trigger_question_examples: What do the lumps look and feel like up close?
Can you see anything inside them, like dots or specks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're rough and hard with a knobbly surface, and if I look closely some of them have tiny black dots like little splinters inside. The normal lines of my skin look broken up where they are.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient has been picking, biting and tweezing at the lesions, which precedes and correlates with the spread (autoinoculation)
trigger_question_examples: Have you been touching, picking or trying to remove them yourself?
What have you tried to do about them so far?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I've been picking at them and even trying to dig them out with tweezers because I want them gone — but they just keep spreading to new spots.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Periungual lesions are painful on pressure, limiting fine-motor tasks such as typing and writing
trigger_question_examples: Are any of them painful, and where?
Do they affect what you can do with your hands?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The ones right next to my nails hurt when I press on them or try to type or hold a pen — that's what's making everyday things hard.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No spontaneous bleeding, no dark/irregular pigmentation, no ulceration, and no single rapidly enlarging lesion — features that would raise concern for skin malignancy are absent
trigger_question_examples: Do any of them bleed on their own, or have they turned black or brown?
Is any single one growing much faster than the others or turning into an open sore?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, none of them bleed by themselves and they haven't gone black or brown. They're all pretty similar — none of them is turning into an open sore or growing way faster than the rest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No surrounding warmth, spreading redness, swelling, pus, or red streaks travelling up the arm, and no fever — no secondary bacterial infection
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin around any of them red, hot, swollen or leaking pus?
Any red streaks running up your hand or arm, or any fever?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the skin around them isn't red or hot or swollen, there's no pus, and I don't have any red streaks up my arm or any fever.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No systemic upset — no fever, no unintentional weight loss, feels well in themselves
trigger_question_examples: Have you felt unwell in yourself, had fevers or lost any weight?
Any other symptoms apart from the lumps?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I feel completely well otherwise — no fevers, no weight loss, nothing like that. It's just the lumps and how worried they've made me.
